edge: Bird’s-eye-view (BEV) representation is widely adopted in modern methods for vision-centric autonomous driving perception to describe a 3D scene.
Bird’s-eye-view (BEV) representation is more efficient than voxel representation in describing a 3D scene.
Tri-perspective view (TPV) representation is proposed as an improvement over bird’s-eye-view (BEV) representation in describing the 3D scene with finer details.
Transformer-based TPV encoder (TPVFormer) is proposed to lift image features to the 3D tri-perspective view (TPV) space effectively.
Attention mechanism is employed in transformer-based TPV encoder (TPVFormer) to aggregate image features corresponding to each query in each TPV plane.
Using only camera inputs can achieve comparable performance with LiDAR-based methods on the LiDAR segmentation task on nuScenes.

Implicit representations learn a continuous function that takes the 3D coordinate of a point and outputs the representation of this point.
Implicit representations and explicit representations have different advantages, with implicit representations enabling arbitrary-resolution modeling and computation-efficient architectures.
Hybrid explicit-implicit representations inject spatial information into the continuous mapping of implicit representations and share computation-efficient architectures with them.
Hybrid explicit-implicit representations have a better spatial awareness of explicit representations and focus on small-scale complex scenes for 3D-aware image rendering.
The proposed approach aims to use implicit representation to model outdoor scenes for 3D surrounding perception in autonomous driving.
Autonomous driving perception requires both expressive and efficient representation of the complex 3D scene.
The proposed approach generalizes Bird's Eye View (BEV) to Top-View Perspective (TPV) for autonomous driving perception.

BEV representation uses a 2D feature map to encode the top view of a scene, while TPV representation involves expressive and efficient representation of the complex 3D scene by preserving the dimensionality of the real world.
Voxel representation describes a 3D scene with dense cubic features, while TPV representation involves expressive and efficient representation of the complex 3D scene.
Voxel representation uses a 3D dense cubic feature description, while BEV representation uses a 2D feature map to encode the top view of a scene.

TPV representation aggregates projections of a point on different views to describe it comprehensively

The TPV planes construct a full-scale 3D feature space through repetition along orthogonal directions and summation. This allows for storage and computation with lower complexity compared to the voxel feature space.

Compared with BEV, TPV planes are perpendicular to each other, which results in diversified point features along orthogonal directions. TPV representation offers a more comprehensive and fine-grained understanding of 3D surroundings while remaining efficient.
TPVFormer is designed for vision-centric autonomous driving perception, highlighting the importance of vision in autonomous driving.
TPVFormer is specifically introduced as a transformer-based encoder for lifting image features to TPV planes, emphasizing its role in image processing.
TPV planes and TPV queries are correlated, as each TPV query corresponds to a grid cell feature in one of the three planes, indicating a direct relationship between the two concepts.
TPV queries encode view-specific information from the corresponding pilar region, showcasing the connection between TPV queries and the specific regions they represent.
The hybrid-cross-attention block (HCAB) in TPVFormer includes both CVHA and ICA attention mechanisms, highlighting the collaboration between these two components for effective image processing.
The HAB block contains only CVHA attention and specializes in contextual information encoding, following HCAB blocks.
TPVFormer is built by stacking N1 HCAB blocks and N2 HAB blocks. TPV queries play a crucial role in the TPVFormer architecture.
TPV queries and TPV planes are used in attention and 3D representation contexts, respectively.
TPV queries are enhanced with raw visual information from image features in HCAB blocks.
TPV queries are refined with contextual clues from other queries in HAB blocks.
TPV queries play a key role in mapping to a 2D grid cell region in the view and further to a 3D pillar region extending from the view.
In TPVFormer, image cross-attention is used to lift multi-scale and possibly multi-camera image features to the TPV planes.
Deformable attention is employed instead of full-scale vanilla cross-attention in TPVFormer for computational efficiency.

TPV representation is able to describe the fine-grained structures of a 3D scene, which is related to voxel in achieving semantic voxel occupancy prediction.

The IoU improves as HCAB blocks increase, validating the importance of direct visual clues for geometry understanding. Semantic prediction relies on both visual and contextual information as the highest mIoU is achieved with a moderate number of HCAB and HAB blocks.

TPVFormer model is based on the attention mechanism to lift image features to the 3D TPV space, demonstrating that the TPVFormer produces consistent semantic voxel occupancy prediction with only sparse point supervision during training.
Current TPVFormer still underperforms LiDAR-based methods for lidar segmentation, showing the comparison between the two methods.
